behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is detector orchestration across local modules (git-state reader + PR-state provider + age-threshold logic). MOCK-MISS is at module handoff, not a single pure function. REAL-INFRA is not explicitly required by this behavior text (unlike behavior 4), so System is not the minimum. LLM-DEP and MULTI-STEP are absent. Rejection gate: rejected System because behavior text does not require live repo/network/subprocess execution; it only specifies correctness of combined conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire detector with an in-process git-state fixture provider and PR-state fixture provider, then assert stale=true only when all three conditions are met (ahead of main, older than threshold, no open PR), and stale=false when any one condition is false.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: This is startup wiring/side-effect behavior: session bootstrap consumes computed branch state and emits a warning payload. MOCK-MISS is cross-module handoff (bootstrap -> detector/status -> renderer/logger), so Unit is too low. No explicit need for real OS/network/subprocess, so System is not minimum. No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run session-start initialization with a stubbed stale branch state and capture emitted warning output/event; assert warning is emitted once and includes actionable context fields (branch name, age, ahead/PR status hint).

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is explicitly a local deterministic classifier function. No module boundary, no I/O, no model output dependency. Higher levels add infrastructure but do not increase failure detection for this specific logic.
behaviors.test_description: Table-drive classifier inputs to expected labels and include repeat-run determinism checks for identical inputs.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text explicitly requires live repo and PR state. REAL-INFRA is required (real git state and real PR service/API path), and mocks can hide integration failures in command execution, auth/state fetch, and rendering under real data. No LLM dependency or multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Execute `/kaizen-wip` in a real test repo setup with real branch divergence and real PR state lookup path; verify rendered output shows `clean` for synced/PR-backed branch and `STALE` for branch with commits not in main and no PR.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: Failure is in business-rule composition between branch-state evaluator and PR-status interpretation (open/merged should suppress stale-no-pr). This is primarily module interaction; real OS/network is not required by behavior text. Rejection gate: rejected System because text does not demand live PR API; deterministic PR status fixtures can expose false-positive logic bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Feed detector/classifier with branch conditions that would otherwise be stale, plus PR statuses `open` and `merged`; assert output is not `stale-no-pr` in both cases.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: Rebase/squash semantics are real VCS graph transformations; failures often appear only with actual git history rewriting and merge-base behavior. MOCK-HIDE risk is high with synthetic commit lists because hash/ancestry edge cases are the core failure mode. No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: In a real git repo test harness, create stale branch, then perform rebase and squash-merge scenarios that change hashes while integrating work; run detector and assert branch is no longer classified `stale-no-pr` after integration.
